segments.s686-g33: چنین هم بماناد is a wish formula: چنین ('thus') + هم ('also') qualifies the wish, and بماناد is the optative/hortative form 'may remain'. سالی here follows the verb and gives a duration; combined with هزار it means 'for a thousand years' (سالی in the numeral sense 'for X years'). Word order is poetic: the adverbial phrase 'for a thousand years' is fronted after the verb phrase.
segments.s686-g34: که introduces a relative clause 'who/that'. از تخمه من is a genitive phrase 'from my stock/offspring' (از + noun). بود is the past copula 'was' (here a lexical past), and شهریار is the predicate noun 'king'. The relative clause describes the person wished to remain.
segments.s686-g35: پسر بر پسر is a stereotyped poetic pluralizing expression meaning 'son upon son' or 'generation after generation' (reduplicative apposition). هم‌چنین 'likewise/similarly' modifies ارجمند 'esteemed/honored'. The adjective ارجمند follows the noun phrase it qualifies in normal Persian order.
segments.s686-g36: بماناد repeats the optative 'may remain'. با introduces an instrumental/associative phrase 'with' (با تاج و تخت بلند) describing the manner or possession: تاج 'crown' and تخت 'throne' joined by و, and بلند 'high/lofty' modifies تخت. The whole phrase qualifies how they should remain (honored with crown and lofty throne).
segments.s686-g37: بآذر مه is an archaic/poetic time expression: ب + آذر مه = 'in the month of Azar' (مه here = 'month'). اندر = 'in/within', and بد 'bad/ill' qualifies the time; و روز هور links 'bad [time]' with 'the day Hūr' (هور here is the proper name/day-name). The whole phrase sets a fixed, ominous time.
segments.s686-g38: که introduces a subordinate clause 'when/that'. از شیر is an ablative 'from the lion' (شیر here is the animal). پردخته شد is a passive/arranged verbal phrase: پردخته (poetic participial/adjunct form meaning 'fashioned/treated/formed') + شد 'became/was made', and پشت گور means 'to/at the back of the grave' or 'the rear of the grave' (پشت as location). The clause is compressed and archaic in syntax; literal reading gives 'when, from the lion, was fashioned to the back of the grave.'
segments.s686-g39: چنین گفت is a simple reporting formula 'thus he said/spoke' (چنین = 'thus'). زان پس is a temporal adverbial 'after that/thereafter' (زان = 'from that', پس = 'after'). بایرانیان is the dative/target group 'to/among the Iranians' (in older Persian the postposition sense is flexible); here it identifies the audience or sphere affected.
segments.s686-g40: که here introduces the content/result clause 'that/reason why': برخاست is the past 'arose/rose up', and its subjects are پرخاش 'reproach/attack' and کین 'hatred/vengeance', joined by و. از میان 'from among' or 'between/among them' indicates that these hostile feelings sprang up in the midst (میان). Overall: 'that reproach and hatred arose from among [them].'
segments.s686-g17: بیامد is the simple past, 3rd-person singular (came) — an archaic/poetic form equivalent to آمد. یکی modifies the following noun as 'a certain' (indefinite determiner). The noun phrase مرد پیروزبخت places the adjective پیروزبخت (fortunate) after the noun, the normal Persian adjective order.
segments.s686-g18: نهاد here is the past-tense verb 'placed/stood' (past of نهادن) meaning 'set/placed himself' or 'took his station'. اندر is an older/prepositional form meaning 'in/within'. ایوان بهرام تخت is a genitival sequence: ایوان (iwan/porch) modified by بهرام (Bahram) + تخت (throne) = 'the iwan of Bahram's throne'.
segments.s686-g19: برفتند is past, 3rd-person plural ('they went/they departed'). ایوان شاهی is a noun + adjective ('the royal iwan'), with شاهی after the noun. چو عاج is a simile ('like ivory') and postpositive, describing the iwan's appearance (or the manner of the scene) — چو here = 'like/as'.
segments.s686-g20: بیاویختند is past, 3rd-person plural ('they hung' or 'they suspended'). از marks source or point ('from'), برگاه is 'royal court/raised platform' (the royal dais), and تاج is the direct object 'crown'. Word order is verb – prepositional phrase – object, typical in classical Persian narrative.
segments.s686-g21: برتخت is a fused prepositional phrase meaning 'on the throne' (بر + تخت). زرین is the adjective 'golden' following the noun. یکی is the indefinite determiner 'a certain' and precedes the noun زیرگاه ('low station/low seat'), so the phrase reads 'on the golden throne (they placed) a certain lowly seat.'
segments.s686-g22: نهادند is the past, 3rd-person plural 'they placed' (here the verb placing the seat). و is the coordinating 'and'. پس is temporal 'then/afterwards'. برگشادند is past plural 'they opened' and راه is the object 'the way/path' — together 'and then they opened the way.'
segments.s686-g23: نشست is past, 3rd-person singular 'he sat' or 'he took his seat.' The classical locative phrase از بر تخت uses از + بر + تخت to indicate position relative to the throne (here idiomatically 'on/upon the throne'); بهرامشاه names whose throne it is ('Bahram-shah'). Word order: verb + locative phrase + possessor noun.
segments.s686-g24: به سر برنهاد is a light prepositional structure: به marks direction 'to/on', سر 'head', and برنهاد (past of برنهادن) 'he placed on'. آن کیانی کلاه is the noun phrase 'that Kayanian hat' with the demonstrative آن ('that') before the adjective کیانی (Kayanian/royal) and the noun کلاه (hat/crown) last — typical Persian modifier order.
segments.s686-g49: هرآنکس که introduces a relative clause: هرآنکس ‘whoever’ + که ‘who/that’, followed by the predicate. با شاه پیوسته بود is a past-state description: پیوسته (here an adjective/adverb ‘attached/constantly present’) takes the copula بود ‘was’ to form ‘was attached to / was constantly with the king.’ The whole segment therefore means ‘whoever was constantly with the king.’
segments.s686-g50: بران پادشاهی is a topical/prepositional phrase (‘with/at that kingship’ or ‘of that kingship’) placed before the experiencer. دلش خسته بود is the main clause: دلش ‘his (their) heart’ (دل + pronominal suffix ش) is the experiencer and خسته بود is a past-state predicate ‘was tired/weary.’ Together: ‘of that kingship, his heart was weary.’
segments.s686-g51: برفتند is the 3rd-person plural past verb ‘they went/departed.’ زان بوم is a prepositional phrase ‘from that land’ (زان = بر آن/from that). تا مرز روم uses تا in the sense ‘as far as’ to mark the extent: ‘as far as the border of Rome.’ The sequence gives a movement clause: ‘They departed from that land as far as the Roman frontier.’
segments.s686-g52: پراگنده گشتند is an adjectival predicate plus past plural verb: پراگنده ‘scattered’ + گشتند ‘they became/were turned’ yields ‘they became scattered.’ ز آباد بوم is a source phrase ‘from the prosperous/inhabited homeland’ (ز = from, آباد = prosperous/inhabited, بوم = homeland). Together: ‘they became scattered from the flourishing land.’
segments.s686-g9: 'که' introduces an explanatory/relative clause ('that'/'who'). The clause is a nominal predication with subject before the copula: 'بهرام شاهست' = 'Bahram is king'; 'شاهست' is the archaic/poetic fused copula 'شاه' + 'ست' (is king). The conjunction 'و' links a second predicate 'پیروزبخت' (elliptically repeating the copula: 'and (is) fortunate').
segments.s686-g10: 'سزاوار' is a predicative adjective meaning 'deserving/worthy (of)'; it takes the following noun 'تاج' as the thing of which one is deserving. The copula 'است' follows to make 'is deserving'. The conjunction 'و' coordinates a parallel object/predicate 'زیبای تخت' (literally 'the beautiful throne'), where the copula is omitted by ellipsis and the adjective 'زیبای' modifies 'تخت'.
segments.s686-g11: 'نجوید' is present-tense with the negative prefix 'ن-' (here meaning 'seeks not / seeks nothing'); followed by 'جز' ('except') this yields 'seeks nothing except...'. 'از راستی' indicates the source/object ('of/from truth'), and 'درجهان' is an adverbial phrase 'in the world' placed after the noun phrase.
segments.s686-g12: The paired 'چه ... و چه ...' is a correlative construction meaning 'whether ... or ...'. 'در آشکار' = 'in the open/manifestly' and 'اندر نهان' = 'in the hidden/secretly'; 'در' and 'اندر' are parallel prepositions marking the locative phrases.
segments.s686-g13: 'نوشته شد' is a passive/perfect construction 'was written' (نوشته as past participle + شد as auxiliary). The following 'آن' (‘that’) is the demonstrative referring to the writing. 'شمع برداشتند' is a separate clause in past tense plural 'they took (up) the candle' ('برداشتند' = they took/removed).
segments.s686-g14: شَب تیره is a noun + adjective 'the dark/gloomy night'. 'باندیشه' is a prepositional/adverbial phrase meaning 'into/with thought' (the b- prefix marking direction/instrument). 'بگذاشتند' is past-tense plural 'they placed/left' (here best read as 'they left [the matter] to contemplation' or 'they committed themselves to thought').
segments.s686-g15: 'چو' here is a temporal conjunction 'when'. 'پنهان شد' ('became hidden') is an intransitive verbal phrase with 'پنهان' (hidden) + 'شد' (became). 'آن چادر لاژورد' is the noun phrase 'that lapis/azure veil/tent' (چادر = veil/tent; لاژورد = lapis/azure) with adjective after the noun.
segments.s686-g16: The main clause 'جهان شد ... زرد' = 'the world became yellow/pale'. 'ز' is the causal/prepositional 'from/because of', and 'دیدار خورشید' ('the seeing/meeting of the sun') indicates the cause — i.e., 'from the sight of the sun'. Word order is subject + copula + causal phrase + adjectival result.
segments.s686-g41: کسی ... نیست is a negated existential construction: 'کسی' here functions as 'no one' and 'نیست' negates existence. 'کوبرین' is a proper-noun modifier (here treated as 'of Kobrin' / 'from Kobrin') and 'همداستان' is a predicate adjective meaning 'of one mind' or 'agreeing'. Word order is Subject–Predicate with the negative copula after the subject.
segments.s686-g42: The repeated اگر ... اگر frames a correlative conditional 'whether ... or ...'. 'کژ' and 'راستان' are opposing adjectives 'crooked/wrong' vs. 'upright/right'. 'باشید' is 2nd-person plural present subjunctive/optative (or polite imperative) meaning 'you be' (here 'you are / you may be'); it agrees with the second-person plural addressees and links the two halves of the correlative.
segments.s686-g43: 'به ایران مباشید' uses the negative imperative/subjunctive 'مباشید' (2nd-person plural) meaning 'do not be / do not remain in'. 'به' indicates the place ('to/in Iran') and 'بیش از سه روز' is a duration phrase 'more than three days' with 'بیش از' governing the following measure 'روز'.
segments.s686-g44: 'چهارم' is an ordinal ('on the fourth') used adverbially; 'چو' here means 'when'. The phrase 'از چرخ گیتی فروز' places the action in a temporal/visual frame: 'چرخ گیتی' = 'the heaven/sky of the world' and 'فروز' is a predicate/adjectival participle 'bright/shining' describing how the sky appears at that time.
segments.s686-g45: 'بر آید' is a jussive/optative form built on آید ('come') with the preposition 'بر' 'toward/onto', functioning as an exhortation 'may it come to pass that ...'. 'همه' is the subject 'everyone' and 'نزد خسرو' is a locative phrase 'in the presence of Khosrow / before Khosrow'. 'شوید' is a second verb in the subjunctive/optative ('become' / 'be') used here as part of the exhortation — together the clause urges that all come to the king.
segments.s686-g46: 'برین بوم' is 'بر این بوم' = 'on this land' (بر + این + بوم); the orthographic split shows the demonstrative as the -ین element. The clause 'بر بیش ازین مغنوید' uses 'بیش ازین' = 'more than this' limiting the action, and 'مغنوید' is a 2nd-person-plural present/imperative form of 'خواندن' used here as an instruction 'sing' (context implies a restriction: 'sing no more than this'). Word order places the locative/demonstrative before the verb for emphasis.
segments.s686-g47: The clause begins with a negation implied by the preceding 'نه' (not shown among the listed words). 'از دل' is an idiom 'from the heart/heartily'; 'برو' here adverbially supports that idiom (a fossilized particle meaning 'forth/heartily'); 'خواندند' is past tense, 3rd-person plural 'they uttered/sang', and 'آفرین' is the interjection 'bravo'. Together the phrase says they did not give genuine applause.
segments.s686-g48: 'که' introduces the explanatory/purposive clause 'that/for that reason'. 'پردخته' is a past-participial adjective 'well‑wrought, well‑made' and stands for 'that which you have fashioned'; 'از تو' 'from/of you' marks the maker; 'مبادا' is a negative jussive meaning 'may it not be / lest'; and 'زمین' is the endpoint 'to the earth' — the clause expresses a fear/wish that the well-made thing of the addressee not fall to the ground.
segments.s686-g25: دبیرش = دبیر + enclitic -ش 'his', here 'his scribe/secretary'. بیاورد is past (bīāvard) 'brought' (3rd person sing.), so the clause reads 'his scribe brought...'. عهد کیان is a genitive/possessive phrase 'the covenant of the Kians' (poetic style often omits an explicit ezāfe), with کیان referring to the royal house/dynasty.
segments.s686-g26: نوشته is a noun 'the writing/inscription' (or 'text'); بران is a locative/adverbial 'on that / thereon' (pointing back to the thing mentioned); پربها modifies پرنیان and means 'costly/precious'; پرنیان is a noun 'silken cloth/robe'. The phrase orders the noun followed by a locative modifier typical of poetic compression: 'the writing on that costly silk.'
segments.s686-g27: گوایی is an adverb 'as if / seemingly'. نوشتند is past plural 'they wrote'. یکسر here is an adverb/quantifier 'entirely / all together' modifying مهان (the company), so 'as if the guests all together wrote...'. Note the flexible word order (adverb + verb + quantified noun) common in poetry.
segments.s686-g28: که introduces a subordinate/content clause 'that...'. بهرام is the subject proper name. شد is the past copula/verb 'became'. شهریار جهان is the predicate 'king of the world' (شهریار = king, جهان = world); word order is Subject + Verb + Predicate in this clause.
segments.s686-g29: بران نامه places the locative phrase 'on that document' up front. چون here functions temporally/concessively 'when / as' (classical usage often reads it as 'when'). نام کردند is a compound verbal expression formed with the noun نام + کردند meaning 'they named / gave the name'. یاد is used adverbially 'as a remembrance / as a memorial' and links this clause to the following action.
segments.s686-g30: بروبر is an adverbial/prepositional particle 'upon it / directly upon' (literally 'face-to-face / over it'). یکی = 'one' (a single). مهر here means 'seal' (stamp). زرین is the adjective 'golden' modifying مهر; poetic style can omit the ezāfe link. نهاد is past 'placed' (put). So the clause reads 'they placed upon it one golden seal.'
segments.s686-g31: چنین گفت = 'thus he said' (چنین 'thus/so', گفت = said). کاین introduces the reported content 'that this...', and پادشاهی مراست is an archaic/clitic formation meaning 'this kingship is mine' where مراست is a fused form (مرا + است) 'belongs to me / is mine' — an idiomatic poetic construction. (Note: some listed dictionary senses for گفت and مراست in the input do not match the verbal senses used here.)
segments.s686-g32: بدین is classical/poetic 'by this' (often written بدین = به + این). بر شما = 'upon/over you' (بر = on/upon, شما = you). پاک یزدان = 'pure God' (پاک 'pure', یزدان 'God' / divine). گواست is an archaic/copula compound meaning 'is witness' (گوا + است), so the whole phrase = 'by this the pure God is witness over you' — an oath formula. Several of the segmented items in the supplied word list (e.g. دین, گواست) do not have matching senses in that list, so their sense index is marked -1.
segments.s686-g1: چوپیدا شد forms a temporal clause: چوپیدا (چو + پیدا) = 'when/when found' and شد is the past of شدن marking appearance/occurrence. آن is the demonstrative 'that' pointing to the noun چادر (here 'tent'); قیرگون is an adjectival predicate 'pitch-black' placed after the noun, the normal poetic word order.
segments.s686-g2: درفشان شد is a predicative sequence: درفشان used adjectivally 'shining/glittering' and شد 'became' gives past-state meaning. اختر is the subject 'star' and بچرخ (infinitival/verb stem 'to revolve') with اندرون 'within/inside' describes its motion or appearance in the interior.
segments.s686-g3: چو introduces a temporal clause 'when'. آواز دارنده is a genitival/attributive phrase: آواز 'call/cry' and دارنده 'bearer' -> 'the bearer of the call' (i.e., the one calling, the sentry). پاس is 'sentry/guard' and خاست is the past 'rose/was raised', so the whole means 'when the sentry's call rose.'
segments.s686-g4: This clause lists requests: قلم خواست — قلم 'pen' (direct object) + خواست (past of خواستن) 'wanted/asked for'. The coordination with و ... قرطاس خواست repeats the verb to mean 'and (he) wanted paper': و is coordinating 'and', قرطاس 'paper/sheet', خواست again 'asked for.' The agent (subject) بہرام appears between the two verbs and governs both requests.
segments.s686-g5: بیامد is the simple past 'came'. دبیر 'secretary/scribe' is the subject, followed by two adjectives coordinated with و: خردمند 'wise' and راد 'generous' (here adjectival, not a proper name). This is a short nominative phrase 'the wise and generous scribe came.'
segments.s686-g6: دوات و قلم lists the objects 'inkpot and pen'. پیش دانا نهاد is a verb phrase: پیش 'before/in the presence of', دانا 'the wise one' (dative/benefactive sense), and نهاد is the past of نهادن 'placed/put'. So the scribe 'placed the inkpot and pen before the wise man.'
segments.s686-g7: بدو گفت is 'he said to him' (بدو = to him). عهدی ز ایرانیان: عهدی is an object 'a covenant/pledge' and ز ایرانیان 'concerning/from the Iranians' uses poetic ز = 'of/from/about'. Together: 'He said to him (that) a covenant about the Iranians...'.
segments.s686-g8: بباید is the modal 'must/ought' (obsolete/poetic present/past modal form). نوشتن is the infinitive 'to write' serving as the complement of بباید. بر + ین (here orthographic برین = بر این) = 'on this', and پرنیان is the silken cloth/robe used as writing material; syntactically: '(it) must be written on this silken cloth.'

sentences.s686-s17: May he thus also remain for a thousand years, who from my seed was a king.
sentences.s686-s18: May son upon son likewise remain honored, with crown and a lofty throne.
sentences.s686-s19: In the month of Azar, in an ill time and the day of Hūr, when from the lion it was fashioned to the back of the grave.
sentences.s686-s20: Thus he spoke; afterwards, among the Iranians rose reproach and hatred.
sentences.s686-s9: A certain fortunate man came and stood in the iwan of Bahram's throne.
sentences.s686-s10: They moved away from the royal iwan, its splendor like ivory; from the royal dais they suspended the crown.
sentences.s686-s11: They set a lowly seat on the golden throne for one, and then they opened the way.
sentences.s686-s12: He sat upon Bahram-shah's throne, and upon his head he set that Kayanian hat.
sentences.s686-s25: Whoever had been constantly with the king grew weary of that kingship.
sentences.s686-s26: They departed from that land as far as the border of Rome; from the prosperous homeland they became scattered.
sentences.s686-s5: For Bahram is king and fortunate, worthy of the crown and of the beautiful throne.
sentences.s686-s6: He seeks nothing but truth in the world, whether in the open or in secret.
sentences.s686-s7: When that writing was finished, they took up the candle and, leaving the dark night to contemplation, withdrew into thought.
sentences.s686-s8: When that lapis-blue veil was hidden, the world turned yellow (grew pale) from the sight of the sun.
sentences.s686-s21: No one of Kobrin is of one mind; whether you are crooked or upright.
sentences.s686-s22: Do not remain in Iran more than three days; on the fourth, when the world's heaven glows, ...
sentences.s686-s23: Let everyone go to Khosrow; on this land, and no more than this, sing.
sentences.s686-s24: They did not, from the heart, cry 'bravo', for lest what you have fashioned fall to the earth.
sentences.s686-s13: His scribe brought the Kian covenant, the writing upon that costly silken cloth.
sentences.s686-s14: It seemed as if all the guests wrote that Bahram became king of the world.
sentences.s686-s15: Upon that document, when they named it, they set as a memorial upon it one golden seal.
sentences.s686-s16: He thus said, 'This kingship is mine; by this, the pure God is witness upon you.'
sentences.s686-s1: When that pitch-black tent was found, the star within became glittering and revolved.
sentences.s686-s2: When the guard's call rose, Bahram asked for a pen and for paper.
sentences.s686-s3: The wise, generous scribe came and placed the inkpot and pen before the sage.
sentences.s686-s4: He said to him, 'A covenant concerning the Iranians must be written on this silken cloth.'
